2606.14420.title: Coping in Crisis: Computational Modeling of Coping Styles in Digital Crisis Discourse During the 2023 Turkiye Earthquake
2606.14420.paper_summary: How do people cope when disaster strikes and can we detect it at scale, in real time, from what they write? This study addresses that question using over one million Turkish-language tweets posted in the aftermath of the February 6, 2023 earthquake in Turkiye, which unfolded in a deeply polarized political context just months before a national election. Drawing on Lazarus and Folkman's (1984) coping theory, we develop a multi-label BERTurk classifier to detect three coping styles (problem-focused, emotion-focused, and meaning-making) across four theoretically motivated crisis phases. BERTurk achieves a macro F1 of 0.693, substantially outperforming a zero-shot mDeBERTa baseline (macro F1 = 0.324). Applied to the full corpus, the classifier reveals a clear temporal trajectory: problem-focused coping dominates the urgency phase and declines sharply, emotion-focused coping rises and stabilizes, and meaning-making increases monotonically. Anger correlates most strongly with meaning-making (Spearman r = 0.387), suggesting it functions as a mobilizing force toward blame attribution rather than practical action. These findings demonstrate that coping theory can be reliably operationalized in real-world digital crisis data and that doing so can help humanitarian organizations tailor their responses to where a population actually is.
2606.14420.paper_summary_zh: 人們在災難來臨時如何應對，我們能否從他們所寫的內容中實時、大規模地檢測到這一點？本研究針對這一問題，使用了超過一百萬條在2023年2月6日土耳其地震後發佈的土耳其語推文，該事件發生在一個深度兩極化的政治背景下，距離全國選舉僅有幾個月。基於拉扎魯斯和福克曼（1984）的應對理論，我們開發了一個多標籤的BERTurk分類器，以檢測四個理論驅動的危機階段中的三種應對風格（以問題為中心、以情感為中心和意義建構）。BERTurk達到了0.693的宏觀F1值，顯著超過了零樣本mDeBERTa基準（宏觀F1 = 0.324）。應用於完整語料庫，該分類器揭示了明確的時間軌跡：以問題為中心的應對在緊急階段佔主導地位並急劇下降，以情感為中心的應對上升並穩定，而意義建構則單調增加。憤怒與意義建構的相關性最強（斯皮爾曼r = 0.387），這表明它作為一種動員力量，更傾向於歸因責任而非實際行動。這些發現表明，應對理論可以在現實世界的數字危機數據中可靠地操作化，這樣做可以幫助人道主義組織根據人口實際情況量身定制其應對措施。
2606.14420.author: Şevval Çakıcı et.al.
